title: galium asprellum var. davuricum
intro: Galium asprellum var. davuricum, also known as Korean Bedstraw, is a plant species in the bedstraw family (Rubiaceae) that is native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive function. Traditionally, the plant was prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes, and it may be used as an alternative remedy for various health conditions.
